zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Cole於1330年出生在英國戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆時，他的父親Sir John Cole25歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Weston21歲。他於大約1356年與Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1380年在英國戴文享年50歲，埋葬在英國戴文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Cole于1330年出生在英国戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆时，他的父亲Sir John Cole25岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Weston21岁。他于大约1356年与Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1380年在英国戴文享年50岁，埋葬在英国戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Cole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colton
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire): habitational name from any of various places called Colton in England. Examples in Norfolk, Staffordshire, and North Yorkshire are from the Old English personal name Cola (or the cognate Old Norse Koli; see Cole 2) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The place so named in Somerset has as its first element the Old English personal name Cūla (of uncertain origin). The one in Cumbria has a river name apparently derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘hazel’. This English name is also common in Ireland; it was the name of a bishop of Derry in 1397. There seems also to have been confusion with Culliton .
Scottish and Irish (Monaghan): shortened and altered form of Gaelic Mac Haldan, ‘son of Haldan’, see Haldane .

variants.name: Colclough
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Cheshire): habitational name from Cowclough in the parish of Whitworth, Lancashire, earlier recorded as Colleclogh, probably named with the Old English byname Cola (see Cole 2) + Old English clōh ‘ravine’.

variants.name: Calcote
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in Bedfordshire, Berkshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Northamptonshire, Warwickshire, and elsewhere) named Caldecote or Caldecott, from Old English cald ‘cold’ + cot ‘cottage, dwelling’. It has been suggested that in Old English this expression denoted an unattended shelter for wayfarers, although in fact some places with this name were of considerable status by 1086, when they appear in Domesday Book. In some instances this and some of the other shortened forms may have arisen from Calcot in Berkshire, Collacott(s) in Devon, or Calcutt in Wiltshire, in all of which the first element apparently comes from the Old English personal name Cola (see Cole 2) or the word col ‘(char)coal’, in which case the meaning would be something like ‘coalshed’.

variants.name: Collignon
meanings: French and Walloon: from a shortened pet form (see Cole and Colle ) of the French personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Colling
meanings: English (northern England and Devon): from the Old Norse personal name Kollungr, a derivative of Koli, or from Old English Colling, a derivative of Cola (see Cole ). Alternatively, from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas (see also Colin ).
German: variant of   Kolling   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Collard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Donat, Normand, Octave, Adelard, Aldor, Andre, Armand, Emile, Germaine, Marcel.
English (southern), French, and Walloon: from a diminutive of the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), a pet form of Nic(h)olas (see Cole ), formed with the Old French suffix -ard.

variants.name: Coale
meanings: English: post-medieval form of   Cole  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Coles
meanings: English (southern): variant of Cole , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Nichol
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from the Middle English personal name Nic(h)ol(e), a vernacular form of Nic(h)olas (see Nicholas ). Compare Nickel and Nicol .

In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’, including Nikolis (see Nicoli ). Compare Nicholas and Nicol .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Cole was born in 1330, in Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England, his father, Sir John Cole, was 25 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Weston, was 21. He married Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway about 1356, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1380, in Devon, England, at the age of 50, and was buried in Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Cole est né en 1330, à Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Cole, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Weston, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway environ 1356, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1380, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Cole 1330 in Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Cole 25 und seine Mutter Lady Elizabeth Weston 21. Er hat um 1356 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1380 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Devon, England und wurde in Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Cole è nato il nel 1330 nel Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir John Cole, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Weston, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway circa 1356, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1380, nel Devon, England, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Coleが1330年にイギリスデボントルバイブリクスハムで生まれた時，父Sir John Coleは25歳，母Lady Elizabeth Westonは21歳であった。約1356年にイギリスイギリスデボンでAnne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythwayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1380年にイギリスデボンで50歳で死去し，イギリスデボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Cole(이)가 1330년 영국 데폰 토르베이 브리스함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Cole(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Weston(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1380년 영국 데폰에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰에 묻혔다.

mn.Of, Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England: Kylgh, Kernev, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Cole nasceu em 1330, em Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Cole, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Weston, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway aproximadamente 1356, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1380, em Devon, Inglaterra, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Cole родился в 1330 г. в местности бриксхам, торбеи, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Cole было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Weston, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway около 1356 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1380 г. в местности девон, Англия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Cole nació en 1330, en Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Cole, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Weston, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Anne de Bodrugan - Heiress of Nythway aproximadamente 1356, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1380, en Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Devon, Inglaterra.
